name: Jacobson's Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: the weak closure of the von Neumann algebra generated by the operator and its commutant

VINIT.chosen_element: unspecified operator T on Hilbert space
VINIT.justification: void state before any algebraic structure is imposed; T exists but its relationships are unmapped

TANCH.chosen_element: the double commutant T'' (the weak closure)
TANCH.justification: terminal anchor that contains and closes the entire algebraic system; Jacobson's theorem states T'' is the weak closure of the algebra generated by T

AFWD.chosen_element: inclusion T ⊆ T' (operator into its commutant)
AFWD.justification: forward morphism: T maps into the space of operators that commute with it; directed transformation toward algebraic structure

AREV.chosen_element: extraction from T'' back to T (descent from closure to generator)
AREV.justification: reverse morphism: the double commutant contains T, so we can extract/recover the original operator from its closure

CLINK.chosen_element: composition of commutation relations: (T ⊆ T') ∘ (T' ⊆ T'')
CLINK.justification: sequential chaining of the commutant hierarchy; each step composes the previous algebraic relationship

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: self-adjoint operators (A = A*) or normal operators (AA* = A*A)
IMSCRIB.justification: identity/self-reference: these operators are characterized by their relationship to themselves; they recognize their own structure

FSPLIT.chosen_element: the operator T splits into two branches: (1) T as a generator, (2) T as an element of T''
FSPLIT.justification: co-multiplication: one operator branches into two distinct roles — the original generator and its manifestation within the double commutant

FFUSE.chosen_element: reconstitution of T from its dual roles: the generator T and its embedding in T'' fuse back to yield T
FFUSE.justification: multiplication: the two branches (T as generator, T in T'') reconstitute the original operator exactly; FFUSE(FSPLIT(T)) = T

EVALT.chosen_element: T is cyclic (generates a dense subspace) or T is irreducible
EVALT.justification: affirmative/success state: the operator exhibits strong algebraic properties; the commutant structure is well-behaved and non-trivial

EVALF.chosen_element: T is reducible or T has a non-trivial invariant subspace
EVALF.justification: negative/failure state: the operator does not generate the full algebra; the commutant is larger than expected, indicating decomposition

ENGAGR.chosen_element: T is simultaneously cyclic and reducible (paradice: both properties held without resolution)
ENGAGR.justification: paradice state: in certain exotic operator configurations, an operator can exhibit both cyclic generation and reducibility; Jacobson's theorem still applies but the interpretation is ambiguous

IFIX.chosen_element: the spectral measure of T (permanent record of T's eigenvalue distribution)
IFIX.justification: ROM fixation: once the spectrum is computed, it is an irreversible, append-only record of T's structure; cannot be undone or modified

phase_2.split_input: operator T on Hilbert space H
split_outputs: T as a generator of the von Neumann algebra W*(T)
T as an element of the double commutant T''
phase_2.fuse_result: the original operator T, recovered exactly from its dual roles

phase_3.void_description: 00 — T is unspecified; no commutant structure has been defined; the operator exists but is algebraically inert
phase_3.true_description: 01 — T is cyclic and irreducible; the commutant T' is abelian; the double commutant T'' equals the weak closure of W*(T); Jacobson's theorem holds with full force
phase_3.false_description: 10 — T is reducible; T has non-trivial invariant subspaces; the commutant T' is non-abelian; the algebra is decomposable
phase_3.both_description: 11 — paradice: T exhibits both cyclic and reducible properties simultaneously; the commutant structure is ambiguous; Jacobson's theorem applies but interpretation is suspended

steps.domain_action: unspecified operator T on Hilbert space H; no algebraic relationships yet defined
T is embedded into the space of bounded linear operators; forward morphism toward structure
composition of T with itself and its adjoint; algebraic operations begin to form
T branches into two roles: (1) generator of the von Neumann algebra W*(T), (2) element of the double commutant T''
T is cyclic — it generates a dense subspace; the T-branch is affirmed; strong algebraic properties hold
forward morphism along the T-branch: T ⊆ W*(T) ⊆ T''
self-reference within the T-branch: T recognizes its own role as a generator; T = T (identity)
T is reducible — it has non-trivial invariant subspaces; the F-branch is affirmed; decomposition occurs
reverse morphism along the F-branch: extraction of T from T'' back toward the original operator; descent from closure
self-reference within the F-branch: T recognizes its role as a reducible element; T = T (identity in reduced form)
the two branches reconstitute T exactly; FFUSE(FSPLIT(T)) = T; the operator is recovered from its dual roles
composition of the fused result with the commutant hierarchy; T ∘ T' ∘ T'' forms a chain
paradice state: T is simultaneously cyclic and reducible; both EVALT and EVALF are held without resolution
the spectral measure of T is permanently recorded; eigenvalue distribution is fixed in ROM; irreversible append-only record
the double commutant T'' is the terminal anchor; it closes and contains the entire algebraic system; Jacobson's theorem is satisfied
descent from T'' back to T; the cycle completes by returning to the original operator
final self-reference: the entire structure recognizes itself as a closed algebraic system; T'' = T'' (identity of the closure)
the theorem statement itself is permanently recorded: 'For any operator T, the weak closure of W*(T) equals T'''; irreversible mathematical fact

phase_5.compiler_frontend: the commutant operation (·)' — translates operator intentions into commutation relations; maps T to T' and T' to T''
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: commutation brackets [T, S] = TS − ST; components communicate by measuring whether they commute or anticommute
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the spectral measure dE_λ of T; state is stored as the distribution of eigenvalues and eigenvectors; retrieved via spectral decomposition
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the weak topology on the operator algebra; determines the order in which limits are taken and when convergence is achieved
phase_5.alfs_store: the Hilbert space H itself; the canonical reference store for all operators; bootstrap store for the entire algebraic structure

phase_6.cycle_cost: one commutation relation evaluation per operation; cost is the computational complexity of checking [T, S] = 0
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: operator T with unknown commutant structure; algebraic relationships unmapped; entropy is maximal (high uncertainty about T')
phase_6.post_cycle_state: operator T with fully determined double commutant T''; commutant structure is known; entropy is minimal (T'' is the unique weak closure)
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the cycle is entropy-conservative because Jacobson's theorem is a tautology in operator algebra: the double commutant is always the weak closure, so no information is created or destroyed; the system merely reveals what was always true

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → AFWD → CLINK → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → IMSCRIB → EVALF → AREV → IMSCRIB → FFUSE → CLINK → ENGAGR → IFIX → TANCH → AREV → IMSCRIB → IFIX
-- Class: Jacobson's Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(11,2,3,2)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=18
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(3, 10)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑾  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑼 → 𐑱  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [2] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑾 → 𐑚  | composition — regime coherence
--   [3] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [4] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [5] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [6] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑚 → 𐑙  | identity — self-imscription
--   [7] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [8] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [9] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑚 → 𐑙  | identity — self-imscription
--   [10] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑱  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [11] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑙 → 𐑳  | composition — regime coherence
--   [12] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑱 → 𐑭  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [13] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑳 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [14] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑗  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [15] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑡 → 𐑠  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [16] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑗 → 𐑭  | identity — self-imscription
--   [17] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑠 → 𐑼  | irreversible fixation — winding number

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def jacobsons_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (unspecified operator T on Hilbert space H; no algebraic relationships yet def...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑼 𐑱)  -- [1] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (T is embedded into the space of bounded linear operators; forward morphism to...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑚)  -- [2] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (composition of T with itself and its adjoint; algebraic operations begin to form)
  -- FSPLIT [3] (gran := 𐑚) (T branches into two roles: (1) generator of the von Neumann algebra W*(T), (2) element of the double commutant T'') / FFUSE [10] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (T is cyclic — it generates a dense subspace; the T-branch is affirmed; strong...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (forward morphism along the T-branch: T ⊆ W*(T) ⊆ T'')
        (.arrow 𐑠 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (self-reference within the T-branch: T recognizes its own role as a generator;...)
      -- F-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (T is reducible — it has non-trivial invariant subspaces; the F-branch is affi...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (reverse morphism along the F-branch: extraction of T from T'' back toward the...)
        (.arrow 𐑠 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (self-reference within the F-branch: T recognizes its role as a reducible elem...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [10]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [10] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (the two branches reconstitute T exactly; FFUSE(FSPLIT(T)) = T; the operator is recovered from its dual roles)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑙 𐑳)  -- [11] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (composition of the fused result with the commutant hierarchy; T ∘ T' ∘ T'' fo...)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑱 𐑭)  -- [12] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (paradice state: T is simultaneously cyclic and reducible; both EVALT and EVAL...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑳 𐑡)  -- [13] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (the spectral measure of T is permanently recorded; eigenvalue distribution is...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑗)  -- [14] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (the double commutant T'' is the terminal anchor; it closes and contains the e...)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑡 𐑠)  -- [15] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (descent from T'' back to T; the cycle completes by returning to the original ...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑗 𐑭)  -- [16] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (final self-reference: the entire structure recognizes itself as a closed alge...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑠 𐑼)  -- [17] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (the theorem statement itself is permanently recorded: 'For any operator T, th...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def jacobsons_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  (jacobsons_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def jacobsons_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  (jacobsons_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem jacobsons_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
